title: Methods providing carrier selection and related network nodes
abstract: According to some embodiments of inventive concepts, a method for carrier selection may be provided in a telecommunications system, with the telecommunications system including at least two network nodes. The method may include determining channel interference of the at least two network nodes, and ranking the at least two network nodes based on the channel interference of the at least two network nodes. Related network nodes are also discussed.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1 . A method for carrier selection in a telecommunications system, the telecommunications system comprising at least two network nodes (BS-1 , BS-2), the method comprising: determining (1301 ) channel interference of the at least two network nodes; and ranking (1303) the at least two network nodes based on the channel interference of the at least two network nodes. 2. The method of Claim 1 further comprising: defining (1305) a set of candidate carriers for channels in the telecommunications system; and providing (1307) a selection for one of the at least two network nodes, of a channel from the set of candidate carriers for channels, wherein the order in which the selection is provided is based on ranking the at least two network nodes. 3. The method of Claim 2, wherein the selected channel is removed from the set of candidate carriers for channels. 4. The method of any of Claims 2-3, wherein the selection for the network node having the higher channel interference is provided before the network node having lower channel interference. 5. The method of any of Claims 1 -4, wherein the telecommunications system comprising the at least two network nodes is operated by a single operator. 6. The method of any of Claims 1 -5, wherein each of the at least two network nodes is an evolved NodeB (eNB). 7. The method of any of Claims 1 -6 wherein determining channel interference of the at least two network nodes comprises determining the channel interference of the at least two network nodes based on unlicensed carriers. 8. The method of any of Claims 2-7 wherein the set of candidate carriers comprises a set of unlicensed candidate carriers. 9. The method of any of Claims 1 -8 further comprising: responsive to ranking the at least two network nodes, signaling (1309) a selection of at least one unlicensed carrier to at least one of the two network nodes. 10. The method of any of Claims 1 -9, wherein the method for carrier selection is a method for coordinated carrier selection (CCS), wherein the telecommunications system is a Licensed Assisted Access (LAA) telecommunications system, and wherein the at least two network nodes are at least two LAA network nodes. 1 1 . A node (BS-n) in a telecommunications system configured to perform operations of any of Embodiments 1 -10. 12. A method in a network node (BS-n) for carrier selection in a telecommunications system, the telecommunications system comprising at least two network nodes (BS-1 , BS-2), the method comprising: receiving (1401 ) estimated numbers of devices per candidate carrier from each of the at least two network nodes, wherein the estimated numbers of devices per candidate carrier are obtained from the at the least two network nodes respectively; and ranking (1403) the candidate carriers based on a consensus of the estimated numbers of devices per candidate carrier. 13. The method of Claim 12, further comprising: receiving (1405) a predicted traffic load from at least one of the network nodes in the telecommunications system. 14. The method of Claim 13, further comprising: assigning (1407) a carrier to the at least one network node based on the ranking of the candidate carriers and on the predicted traffic load. 15. The method of Claim 14, further comprising: signaling (1409) an indication of the assigned carrier to the at least one network node. 16. The method of any of Claims 12-15, wherein the estimated numbers of devices per candidate carrier is an estimated number of devices per candidate carrier operating outside the telecommunications system. 17. The method of any of Claims 12-15, wherein the estimated numbers of devices per candidate carrier is an estimated number of Wi-Fi devices per candidate carrier. 18. The method of any of Claims 12-17, wherein each of the at least two network nodes is an evolved NodeB (eNB). 19. The method of any of Claims 12-18 wherein each of the candidate carriers is an unlicensed candidate carrier. 20. The method of any of Claims 12-19, wherein the method for carrier selection is a method for coordinated carrier selection (CCS), wherein the telecommunications system is a Licensed Assisted Access (LAA) telecommunications system, , and wherein the at least two network nodes are at least two LAA network nodes. 21 . A carrier selection method in a telecommunications system comprising at least two network nodes (BS-1 , BS-2), the method comprising: receiving (1501 ) information related to a number of channels needed for each of the at least two network nodes; ranking (1503) the at least two network nodes based on the number of channels needed for each of the at least two network nodes; and allocating (1505) a number of channels to a first one of the at least two network nodes that indicates a greatest need for channels such that a sum of adjacent channel leakage to other channels is reduced. 22. The method of Claim 19 further comprising: signaling (1507) an indication of the allocated number of channels to the first network node. 23. The method of any of Claims 21 -22 wherein allocating a number of channels comprises allocating the number of channels from a set of channels so that the number of channels are within a bandwidth, and so that all other channels of the set are outside the bandwidth. 24. The method of Claim 23 wherein the wherein the set of channels comprise a set of unlicensed channels. 25. The method of any of Claims 21 -24, wherein each of the at least two network nodes is an evolved NodeB (eNB). 26. The method of any of Claims 21 -25, wherein the carrier selection method is a coordinated carrier selection (CCS) method, wherein the telecommunications system is a Licensed Assisted Access (LAA) telecommunications system, and wherein the at least two network nodes are at least two LAA network nodes. 27. A node in a telecommunications system configured to perform operations of any of Claims 21 -26. 28. A node (BS-n) providing carrier selection in a telecommunications system, the telecommunications system comprising at least two network nodes (BS- 1 , BS-2), wherein the node is adapted to: determine channel interference of the at least two network nodes; and rank the at least two network nodes based on the channel interference of the at least two network nodes. 29. A node (BS-n) providing carrier selection in a telecommunications system, the telecommunications system comprising at least two network nodes (BS-1 , BS-2), wherein the node is adapted to: 

 receive estimated numbers of devices per candidate carrier from each of the at least two network nodes, wherein the estimated numbers of devices per candidate carrier are obtained from the at the least two network nodes respectively; and rank the candidate carriers based on a consensus of the estimated numbers of devices per candidate carrier. 30. A node (BS-n) providing carrier selection in a telecommunications system comprising at least two network nodes (BS-1 , BS-2), wherein the node is adapted to: receive information related to a number of channels needed for each of the at least two network nodes; rank the at least two network nodes based on the number of channels needed for each of the at least two network nodes; and allocate a number of channels to a first one of the at least two network nodes that indicates a greatest need for channels such that a sum of adjacent channel leakage to other channels is reduced. 31 . A node (BS-n) providing carrier selection in a telecommunications system, the telecommunications system comprising at least two network nodes, the node comprising: a network interface (1 105) configured to provide communication with other nodes of the telecommunications system; and a processor (1 103) coupled with the network interface, wherein the processor is configured to, determine channel interference of the at least two network nodes, and rank the at least two network nodes based on the channel interference of the at least two network nodes. 32. A node (BS-n) providing carrier selection in a telecommunications system, the telecommunications system comprising at least two network nodes (BS- 1 , BS-2), the node comprising: a network interface (1 105) configured to provide communication with other nodes of the telecommunications system; and a processor (1 103) coupled with the network interface, wherein the processor is configured to, 

 receive estimated numbers of devices per candidate carrier from each of the at least two network nodes through the network interface, wherein the estimated numbers of devices per candidate carrier are obtained from the at least two network nodes respectively, and rank the candidate carriers based on a consensus of the estimated numbers of devices per candidate carrier. 33. A node (BS-n) providing carrier selection in a telecommunications system comprising at least two network nodes (BS-1 , BS-2), the node comprising: a network interface (1 105) configured to provide communication with other nodes of the telecommunications system; and a processor (1 103) coupled with the network interface, wherein the processor is configured to, receive information related to a number of channels needed for each of the at least two network nodes through the network interface, rank the at least two network nodes based on the number of channels needed for each of the at least two network nodes, and allocate a number of channels to a first one of the at least two network nodes that indicates a greatest need for channels such that a sum of adjacent channel leakage to other channels is reduced. 34. A node (BS-n) providing carrier selection in a telecommunications system, the telecommunications system comprising at least two network nodes (BS- 1 , BS-2), the node comprising: a determining module adapted to determine channel interference of the at least two network nodes; and a ranking module adapted to rank the at least two network nodes based on the channel interference of the at least two network nodes. 35. A node (BS-n) providing carrier selection in a telecommunications system, the telecommunications system comprising at least two network nodes, the node comprising: a receiving module adapted to receive estimated numbers of devices per candidate carrier from each of the at least two network nodes, wherein the estimated 

 numbers of devices per candidate carrier are obtained from the at the least two network nodes respectively; and a ranking module adapted to rank the candidate carriers based on a consensus of the estimated numbers of devices per candidate carrier. 36. A node (BS-n) providing carrier selection in a telecommunications system comprising at least two network nodes (BS-1 , BS-2), the node comprising: a receiving module adapted to receive information related to a number of channels needed for each of the at least two network nodes; a ranking module adapted to rank the at least two network nodes based on the number of channels needed for each of the at least two network nodes; and an allocating module adapted to allocate a number of channels to a first one of the at least two network nodes that indicates a greatest need for channels such that a sum of adjacent channel leakage to other channels is reduced.
